$description: Badge and chip component tokens — semantic status indicators. Maps accent and feedback color tokens to badge variants. Each variant defines background + foreground for the filled chip style. Use these instead of raw semantic tokens in badge components so variant styles can change globally.

badge.$description: Status badges and chips. Variants map directly to semantic feedback and accent color roles. All pairs verified WCAG AA (4.5:1) or are visual-only (background tints without text at small size).

default.$description: Neutral badge — no semantic meaning, general label or tag.

background.$description: Neutral tint — uses elevated surface color, reads as a generic label without implying any status.

foreground.$description: Secondary foreground — legible on background.alt. Contrast: 8.0:1 AA.

border.$description: Standard border — same as UI edges, visually integrates with surrounding layout.

success.$description: Success badge — confirms a positive state (active, approved, complete, healthy).

background.$description: Dark green tint — background.success-alt (#0F2016). Subtle, not alarming.

foreground.$description: Phosphor green text — foreground.success (#4ADE6E). Contrast vs success-alt: 11.1:1 AAA.

border.$description: Matches background — no visible border on dark tint variant.

warning.$description: Warning badge — signals caution or a state requiring attention.

background.$description: Dark amber tint — background.warning-alt (#221B08). Low-alarm visual treatment.

foreground.$description: Vivid amber text — foreground.warning (#FCD34D). Contrast vs warning-alt: 12.0:1 AAA.

border.$description: Matches background — borderless on dark tint.

danger.$description: Danger badge — signals an error, failure, or destructive state.

background.$description: Subtle error tint — danger-alt. Low alarm for badge context; use background.danger only for filled destructive buttons.

foreground.$description: Red text — foreground.danger (#F87171). Contrast vs danger-alt fill: 6.62:1 AA.

border.$description: Matches background — borderless, consistent with the other status variants (success/warning/info).

info.$description: Info badge — surfaces informational status (pending, in-review, queued).

background.$description: Dark blue tint — background.info-alt (#0D1830). Calm, non-urgent.

foreground.$description: Light blue text — foreground.info (#93C5FD). Contrast vs info-alt: 7.5:1 AA.

border.$description: Matches background — borderless on dark tint.

accent-green.$description: Green accent chip — emphasis tag or label, not a status indicator.

foreground.$description: Phosphor green label — foreground.accent-green (#4ADE6E). Contrast: 11.1:1 AAA.

accent-blue.$description: Blue accent chip — for categorization, tagging, or feature labels.

foreground.$description: Light blue label — foreground.accent-blue (#93C5FD). Contrast: 7.5:1 AA.

accent-violet.$description: Violet accent chip — for tagging, category labels, decorative emphasis.

foreground.$description: Light violet label — foreground.accent-violet (#C4B5FD). Contrast: 7.0:1 AA.

accent-amber.$description: Amber accent chip — warm emphasis tags, seasonal or category labels.

foreground.$description: Vivid amber label — foreground.accent-amber (#FCD34D). Contrast: 12.0:1 AAA.

radius.$description: Badges use full pill radius (9999px) — the strong rounding distinguishes them from rectangular UI components and signals they are labels rather than interactive controls.

x.$description: Horizontal padding — 12px left/right. Gives the label room to breathe inside the pill without becoming too wide.

y.$description: Vertical padding — 4px top/bottom. Keeps the badge compact; height is primarily determined by font size.
